summary_text:
S:
The patient reports a right knee pain following a fall while working on his property, describing the pain as exacerbated with flexion and extension, and a palpable pop. He rates the pain a 4-5 out of 10 with movement. He reports some swelling and effusion in the knee. He has been taking ibuprofen 800mg twice daily for the swelling and pain. He admits his diet has been out of control since the pandemic and needs help with sodium reduction.

O:
Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee, pain with flexion and extension, and edema with effusion. The right knee exam reveals a grade 2 systolic ejection murmur. Lung exam is clear bilaterally. Blood pressure was 150/70. The patient reports no chest or abdominal pain or shortness of breath.

A:
The patient has an MCL strain secondary to a traumatic injury.

P:
Rescribe ibuprofen 800mg twice daily. Order a knee brace. Refer to nutrition counseling. Continue lisinopril 20mg daily. Advise patient to elevate leg and limit activity. Schedule follow-up in 2 weeks to assess healing.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Right knee pain.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath.
Gastrointestinal: Denies abdominal pain.
Musculoskeletal: Reports right knee pain.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No wheezes.

Cardiovascular
- Auscultation of Heart: Grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur. Some edema and effusion noted around the right knee.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination: Right knee
- Palpation: Pain to palpation of the medial aspect. No pain to palpation of the lateral aspect.
- ROM: Pain with flexion and extension.
-Some effusion noted around the right knee.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right knee were taken. These show no fractures or bony abnormalities.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Right knee MCL strain.
- Medical Reasoning: Based on the physical examination findings, the patient has a MCL strain of the right knee. - Medical Treatment: I have prescribed ibuprofen 800 mg twice a day. I will also place him in a knee brace. I advised him to ice the knee for 20 minutes at a time for 3 to 4 times a day. The patient was instructed to elevate his leg as needed and avoid strenuous activities for 2-3 weeks.

2. Hypertension.
- Medical Treatment: The patient will continue lisinopril 20 mg daily. I have also provided a referral to see a nutritionist for dietary changes.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports some swelling and effusion in the knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Effusion is typically an observed/clinically assessed finding; however, the patient could be describing visible swelling, so this is a softer organizational issue.

section_miscategorizations.text: The right knee exam reveals a grade 2 systolic ejection murmur.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A systolic ejection murmur is a cardiac finding and should be documented under a cardiac or general exam subsection, not within the knee exam description. This is an internal organization issue within O, not a cross-section error.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports no chest or abdominal pain or shortness of breath.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported review of systems statement and belongs in the Subjective section, not in Objective findings.

The intent is a treatment order (appropriate for Plan), but the wording is ambiguous; clarifying that this is a new or continued prescription would better distinguish it from current-medication documentation.

Although this references an existing medication, it is framed as an explicit management decision (to continue), which is appropriate for the Plan; no change of section is needed, but it borders on current-medication documentation.
